sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for NES version

special_mechanics.name: Marble Rolling Physics
special_mechanics.description: Player-controlled marble navigates isometric maze-like courses. Control mechanism in arcade version uses trackball; NES version uses directional pad. Marble physics influenced by playfield contours and track surfaces that affect control difficulty.

special_mechanics.name: Course Obstacles
special_mechanics.description: Courses populated with various objects and enemies designed to obstruct player movement. Difficulty increases progressively through six courses with varying surface types affecting marble control.

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Empirical/Qualitative data only. No precise physics parameters (velocity, acceleration, gravity values) documented in available sources. Sources focus on development history, gameplay mechanics description, and release information rather than technical movement specifications.
